chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يُسْتَحَبُّ أَنْ يُفْطَرَ عَلَيْهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 66 – What is recommended for one to break a Fast upon it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور لكنه قوي - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/374)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور لكنه قوي - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/374)
text: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) يُفْطِرُ عَلَى التَّمْرِ فِي زَمَنِ التَّمْرِ وَ عَلَى الرُّطَبِ فِي زَمَنِ الرُّطَبِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibrahim Bin Mihzan, from Talha Bin Zayd,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> used to break a Fast upon the dates during the season of the dates, and upon fresh dates during the season of fresh dates’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Ibrahim ibn Mehzam from Talhah ibn Zayd who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, would use dates for breakfast after sunset, if it was the season of dates, or fresh dates if it was the season of fresh dates.”
